description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with two prominent chimneys and a light gray finish. The front facade is characterized by a large central window arrangement with triangular transom windows above, flanked by smaller windows and a covered porch area supported by columns. The exterior walls are clad in light tan brick, and the house includes a side entry door and a sheltered porch area. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with a mix of large and small panes, and the rear of the house features a more modest window and door configuration. The structure appears to be two stories tall, with a spacious attic or loft area under the roof. The overall design is reminiscent of a modern Craftsman style with clean lines and functional outdoor spaces.
description: A two-story tan brick house with a steep gable roof, large central windows, and a covered porch supported by columns. The design features asymmetrical window placement and a modern Craftsman aesthetic.
description_view_0: The front view shows a large central window with triangular transom, a covered porch on the right, and a single blue-framed window on the left.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the deep gable roof, the covered porch with supporting columns, and extensive upper-level windows.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a smaller window, a side entry door, and a continuation of the covered porch, with a simpler window arrangement.
description_view_3: The opposite side view features two upper windows, a lower entry door, and a single blue-framed window, with the gable roof and chimney visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large central picture windows and smaller side windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch supported by columns, with a simple door and overhanging roof for shelter.
